summary: Biggest LIVE risk this cycle is operational, not a filing: the nightly audit shows two HELD names' price caches badly desynced from the broker (WRAP cache 1.555 vs broker/alpaca 2.18 = 38.9% drift, last cached 7/8; NTSK cache 12.61 vs broker 14.4 = 14.2%), and WRAP's stop line ($2.03) sits ABOVE the stale cache value -- any stop-check path reading that cache could phantom-sell a PROFITABLE position at a fake loss, so reconciler/human must confirm the live-price feed before the next stop evaluation; underneath that, the standing distribution overhang persists (UUUU $300M ATM undrawn, WRAP $200M baby-shelf, NTSK post-lockup Form 144) with NO fresh July offering on any held name, and last cycle's DVLT ledger/broker desync is now RESOLVED (broker reports 5 positions, all reconcile to ledger, DVLT closed out).

WRAP.note: Raised on DATA QUALITY: nightly audit flags HELD WRAP broker_disagrees 38.9% (cache 1.555 last 7/8 vs broker 2.18 / alpaca 2.19) + stale_cache -- stop line $2.03 sits ABOVE the stale 1.555, so a stop-check on the cache could phantom-sell this +$3.11 winner at a fake loss; dashboard/exit-radar themselves use the correct 2.19. Dilution: $200M universal baby-shelf standing on a sub-$100M post-pump name = drip CAPACITY only; EDGAR re-check confirms NO July 424B5 draw. Insider activity recent is BUYING (CEO). FinBERT +0.019 (unusual-volume item is noise). Position +$3.11 at $2.18.

UUUU.note: $300M ATM (Controlled Equity Offering, 6/13 program) confirmed still undrawn -- no July prospectus draw. Insider picture net-NEGATIVE by size: Chalmers Form 144 stack via RBC (~800k sh 6/29-7/9, ~$10M+) outweighs CEO Bhappu's 74k/$968k buy (7/7) + Hansen (7/8). Lorentzian k-NN -0.75, FinBERT 0.0. Position -$1.18 at $13.30.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no shelf/ATM). Prior 7/14 broker_disagrees has RESOLVED -- CAI not in tonight's audit; broker $17.36 vs cache converged. FinBERT +0.861 (best +0.861 PTEN/FDA item). Position +$0.65 at $17.36.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$7.09 at $14.49 on an intact breakout, but RAISED-note on DATA QUALITY: audit flags HELD broker_disagrees 14.2% (cache 12.61 vs broker 14.4) -- stops/P&L off the cache would understate the position; live feed shows correct 14.49. Post-IPO-lockup distribution persists (Form 144 1.75M Class A 7/8 + Lightspeed conversions 7/9); ICONIQ 610k buy only partly offsets. EDGAR: NO 424B5/secondary. Hiring pulse healthy (148, ~flat).

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by anti-dilutive $140M self-tender. Audit shows only a minor 3.9% broker_disagrees (cache 5.22 vs broker 5.43) -- immaterial to the $5.00 stop. Hiring pulse 25, flat. Position +$0.75 at $5.49.

flags.why: New top-3 non-held scan name (5.17, below the 6.25 line) with real, recent dilution: priced a $300M underwritten offering -- 23,871,526 sh at $11.52 + 2,170,138 pre-funded warrants -- closed ~5/11/2026. Stock ~$9.35 now sits BELOW the $11.52 offer; the insider +4.0 driving the score is almost certainly offering-backer/healthcare-fund accumulation, NOT management conviction. Do not buy as a fresh insider breakout.

Distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Hiring pulse now 0 open roles (was null). Still on the scan (4.56) and the daily-research avoid list.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces on the scan.

Scan candidate (6.17, just below line) that diluted ~30%: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.70 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's 7/7 buy came AFTER the raise. No NEW July offering. Intel concurs (flags_for_bot).

Scan candidate (6.1, below line) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.59, no fundamental driver. Reject on the pump veto; nightly audit also flags cache stale. Intel concurs (flags_for_bot).

Scan candidate (4.46) on the avoid list with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M sh out, active $300M S-3 shelf (7/8), 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved.
Scan candidate (4.08) on the avoid list: automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup.

On the daily watchlist (scan 3.71) but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, earliest close ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk; intel concurs do-not-chase.

Back on the daily watchlist; priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst. ~$10.22 sits above mean analyst PT. FinBERT -0.027 (worst -0.941 on a -9% day). Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

Off the scan this cycle but still on intel's flags_for_bot: 12-for-1 reverse split effective 7/14 (new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 compliance on an unfunded AI+blockchain+crypto pivot pump. Nightly audit shows a reverse-split cache desync (cache 0.386 vs yahoo 6.04). FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline. Keep vetoed if it resurfaces.
On the desk avoid list, not held: live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83 -- ongoing drip-dilution.

On the avoid list, not held: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround.

No longer held and off the scan -- last cycle's ledger-only broker desync is now RESOLVED (broker reports 5 positions, all reconcile, DVLT closed out). Retained as a do-not-REBUY: still on the desk avoid list as a serial diluter with active pump activity (Mandela Dollar stablecoin 7/10, Dream Bowl meme-coin 7/6). FinBERT +0.455 on GPU-fleet pump headlines.

disagreements: DATA QUALITY (not in intel): the nightly audit flags two HELD names' price caches badly stale vs the broker of record -- WRAP cache 1.555 (last 7/8) vs broker 2.18 = 38.9% drift, NTSK cache 12.61 vs broker 14.4 = 14.2%. WRAP's stop line $2.03 is ABOVE the stale cache value, so a stop evaluation on that cache could phantom-sell a profitable position; intel's buy-side view misses this. Reconciler/human must confirm the live-price feed feeds the stop logic BEFORE the next stop check.

DVLT (was my top reconciliation flag last cycle): RESOLVED. broker_state.json now reports 5 positions all reconciling to the ledger and DVLT is gone -- the ledger/broker desync closed out. Downgraded from a reconciliation escalation to a plain do-not-rebuy (still an avoid-list pump). Daily-research still narrates DVLT as a live 18.28-sh ledger-only position; that note is now stale.
Overtrading (carried, unchanged): radar shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- more than double the 4-buy weekly cap. Standalone discipline flag independent of any single ticker.

UUUU: intel frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently and carries 'no boost'; verified again the Chalmers Form 144 SALE stack (~800k sh 6/29-7/9 via RBC, ~$10M+) outweighs the ~$1M of buying by size, so the insider signal behind the score is net-NEGATIVE -- reinforces my elevated grade.

ARTV (new): intel does not cover it; the +4.0 insider score driving its 5.17 scan rank is offering-backer/healthcare-fund accumulation into a $300M raise that the stock is now underwater on (~$9.35 vs $11.52 offer), NOT management conviction -- treat as dilution overhang, not a buy signal.

AVO: intel carries it as a top non-held name on a +5.0 insider score, but that signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack takeover-style accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan, NOT management conviction; below the buy line so no flag, but the insider component is mis-attributed.
